payload.objective: Review the generated inbox runner together with its reviewed delegate script from the local inbox pipeline and provide a structured verdict using one of: pass, fail, needs_revision. Always output a review markdown artifact and include verdict in metadata.

params.title: BL-20260324-014 live preview smoke sample 2026-03-24 (best-effort reviewable attempt)
params.description:
Purpose:

  Controlled Trello live preview smoke for openclaw-team.

‌

  Expected behavior:

- read-only Trello ingest

- preview creation smoke only

- no business execution claim

- no Trello writeback expected in this step

‌

  Traceability:

- backlog: BL-20260324-014

- blocker context: BL-20260324-015

- created_by: Oscarling

- created_at: 2026-03-24 Asia/Shanghai

‌

  Note:

  This card is only for governed smoke verification and should remain open until the smoke is finished.

Execution contract: treat this as a best-effort, evidence-backed PDF extraction/conversion attempt. Do not claim OCR success without evidence. If full OCR/Excel conversion is not honestly achievable, return reviewable intermediate artifacts, explicit limitations, and next-step guidance. Keep behavior deterministic and limited to declared local artifacts.

review_scope.goal: Audit the wrapper and the reviewed delegate together so the review evidence can speak to the end-to-end readonly smoke path.

artifact_snapshots.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any


DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 900


@dataclass
class RequestContext:
    input_dir: Path
    output_xlsx: Path
    ocr: str
    dry_run: bool
    origin_id: str
    title: str
    description: str
    labels: list[str]
    delegate_path: Path
    timeout_seconds: int


def _expand_path(raw: str) -> Path:
    return Path(os.path.expandvars(os.path.expanduser(raw))).resolve()


def _repo_root_from_script() -> Path:
    return Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]


def _resolve_delegate(preferred_base_script: str | None) -> Path:
    repo_root = _repo_root_from_script()
    script_dir = Path(__file__).resolve().parent

    candidates: list[Path] = []
    if preferred_base_script:
        p = Path(preferred_base_script)
        if p.is_absolute():
            candidates.append(p)
        else:
            candidates.append((repo_root / p).resolve())
            candidates.append((script_dir / p).resolve())
    candidates.append((repo_root / "artifacts/scripts/pdf_to_excel_ocr.py").resolve())

    seen: set[Path] = set()
    for candidate in candidates:
        if candidate in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(candidate)
        if candidate.exists() and candidate.is_file():
            return candidate
    raise FileNotFoundError(
        "Unable to locate reviewed delegate script artifacts/scripts/pdf_to_excel_ocr.py"
    )


def _discover_pdfs(input_dir: Path) -> list[Path]:
    if not input_dir.exists() or not input_dir.is_dir():
        return []
    return sorted(
        [p for p in input_dir.rglob("*") if p.is_file() and p.suffix.lower() == ".pdf"]
    )


def _is_probably_json(text: str) -> bool:
    stripped = text.strip()
    return stripped.startswith("{") and stripped.endswith("}")


def _parse_first_json_object(stdout_text: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
    text = stdout_text.strip()
    if not text:
        return None
    if _is_probably_json(text):
        try:
            data = json.loads(text)
            if isinstance(data, dict):
                return data
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            pass

    decoder = json.JSONDecoder()
    for idx, ch in enumerate(stdout_text):
        if ch != "{":
            continue
        try:
            obj, end = decoder.raw_decode(stdout_text[idx:])
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            continue
        if isinstance(obj, dict):
            return obj
    return None


def _load_report_from_sidecar(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
    if not path.exists() or not path.is_file():
        return None
    try:
        data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    except Exception:
        return None
    return data if isinstance(data, dict) else None


def _normalize_counter(value: Any) -> dict[str, int]:
    if not isinstance(value, dict):
        return {}
    normalized: dict[str, int] = {}
    for k, v in value.items():
        if isinstance(v, bool):
            normalized[str(k)] = int(v)
        elif isinstance(v, int):
            normalized[str(k)] = v
        else:
            try:
                normalized[str(k)] = int(v)
            except Exception:
                continue
    return normalized


def _build_summary(
    status: str,
    ctx: RequestContext,
    pdf_count: int,
    delegated: bool,
    output_exists: bool,
    delegate_report: dict[str, Any] | None,
    delegate_returncode: int | None,
    stdout_tail: str,
    stderr_tail: str,
    notes: list[str],
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    return {
        "status": status,
        "request": {
            "origin_id": ctx.origin_id,
            "title": ctx.title,
            "description": ctx.description,
            "labels": ctx.labels,
            "input_dir": str(ctx.input_dir),
            "output_xlsx": str(ctx.output_xlsx),
            "ocr": ctx.ocr,
            "dry_run": ctx.dry_run,
        },
        "execution": {
            "delegated": delegated,
            "delegate_path": str(ctx.delegate_path),
            "timeout_seconds": ctx.timeout_seconds,
            "delegate_returncode": delegate_returncode,
            "pdf_discovered": pdf_count,
            "output_exists": output_exists,
        },
        "delegate_report": delegate_report,
        "stdout_tail": stdout_tail,
        "stderr_tail": stderr_tail,
        "notes": notes,
    }


def _success_from_delegate(report: dict[str, Any] | None, output_xlsx: Path) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
    reasons: list[str] = []
    if not isinstance(report, dict):
        reasons.append("delegate report missing")
        return False, reasons

    status = report.get("status")
    total_files = report.get("total_files")
    dry_run = report.get("dry_run")
    counter = _normalize_counter(report.get("status_counter"))

    if status != "success":
        reasons.append(f"delegate status is {status!r}, not 'success'")
    if isinstance(total_files, bool) or not isinstance(total_files, int) or total_files < 1:
        reasons.append("delegate total_files does not confirm at least one input")
    if bool(dry_run):
        reasons.append("delegate reports dry_run=true")
    if counter.get("failed", 0) > 0:
        reasons.append("delegate reports failed files")
    processed_evidence = counter.get("success", 0) + counter.get("partial", 0)
    if processed_evidence < 1 and isinstance(total_files, int) and total_files > 0:
        reasons.append("delegate status_counter lacks positive processed evidence")
    if not output_xlsx.exists() or not output_xlsx.is_file():
        reasons.append("expected XLSX output artifact does not exist")
    elif output_xlsx.suffix.lower() == ".xlsx" and output_xlsx.stat().st_size <= 0:
        reasons.append("expected XLSX output artifact is empty")

    return (len(reasons) == 0, reasons)


def _parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Readonly reviewable wrapper for reviewed PDF-to-Excel delegate."
    )
    parser.add_argument("--input-dir", required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--output-xlsx", required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--ocr", default="auto")
    parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true")
    parser.add_argument("--origin-id", default="")
    parser.add_argument("--title", default="")
    parser.add_argument("--description", default="")
    parser.add_argument("--labels", nargs="*", default=[])
    parser.add_argument("--preferred-base-script", default="artifacts/scripts/pdf_to_excel_ocr.py")
    parser.add_argument("--timeout-seconds", type=int, default=DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
    return parser.parse_args()


def main() -> int:
    args = _parse_args()
    try:
        delegate_path = _resolve_delegate(args.preferred_base_script)
    except Exception as exc:
        summary = {
            "status": "failed",
            "request": {
                "origin_id": args.origin_id,
                "title": args.title,
                "description": args.description,
                "labels": args.labels,
                "input_dir": str(_expand_path(args.input_dir)),
                "output_xlsx": str(_expand_path(args.output_xlsx)),
                "ocr": args.ocr,
                "dry_run": args.dry_run,
            },
            "execution": {
                "delegated": False,
                "delegate_path": None,
                "timeout_seconds": args.timeout_seconds,
                "delegate_returncode": None,
                "pdf_discovered": 0,
                "output_exists": False,
            },
            "delegate_report": None,
            "stdout_tail": "",
            "stderr_tail": "",
            "notes": [f"delegate resolution failed: {exc}"],
        }
        print(json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 2

    ctx = RequestContext(
        input_dir=_expand_path(args.input_dir),
        output_xlsx=_expand_path(args.output_xlsx),
        ocr=args.ocr,
        dry_run=bool(args.dry_run),
        origin_id=args.origin_id,
        title=args.title,
        description=args.description,
        labels=list(args.labels),
        delegate_path=delegate_path,
        timeout_seconds=max(1, int(args.timeout_seconds)),
    )

    pdfs = _discover_pdfs(ctx.input_dir)

    if ctx.dry_run:
        summary = _build_summary(
            status="partial",
            ctx=ctx,
            pdf_count=len(pdfs),
            delegated=False,
            output_exists=ctx.output_xlsx.exists(),
            delegate_report=None,
            delegate_returncode=None,
            stdout_tail="",
            stderr_tail="",
            notes=[
                "dry-run requested; wrapper short-circuited before delegate execution",
                "no XLSX artifact claimed",
            ],
        )
        print(json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 0

    if not pdfs:
        summary = _build_summary(
            status="partial",
            ctx=ctx,
            pdf_count=0,
            delegated=False,
            output_exists=ctx.output_xlsx.exists(),
            delegate_report=None,
            delegate_returncode=None,
            stdout_tail="",
            stderr_tail="",
            notes=[
                "no PDF files discovered under input_dir",
                "no XLSX artifact claimed",
            ],
        )
        print(json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 0

    ctx.output_xlsx.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(prefix="pdf_to_excel_delegate_report_", suffix=".json", delete=False) as tf:
        report_path = Path(tf.name)

    cmd = [
        sys.executable,
        str(ctx.delegate_path),
        "--input-dir",
        str(ctx.input_dir),
        "--output-xlsx",
        str(ctx.output_xlsx),
        "--ocr",
        ctx.ocr,
    ]

    # Best-effort compatibility with reviewed delegate implementations that support sidecar reporting.
    cmd.extend(["--report-json", str(report_path)])

    completed: subprocess.CompletedProcess[str] | None = None
    stdout_text = ""
    stderr_text = ""
    delegate_report: dict[str, Any] | None = None

    try:
        completed = subprocess.run(
            cmd,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            timeout=ctx.timeout_seconds,
            check=False,
        )
        stdout_text = completed.stdout or ""
        stderr_text = completed.stderr or ""
        delegate_report = _parse_first_json_object(stdout_text)
        if delegate_report is None:
            delegate_report = _load_report_from_sidecar(report_path)
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:
        stdout_text = (exc.stdout or "") if isinstance(exc.stdout, str) else ""
        stderr_text = (exc.stderr or "") if isinstance(exc.stderr, str) else ""
        summary = _build_summary(
            status="failed",
            ctx=ctx,
            pdf_count=len(pdfs),
            delegated=True,
            output_exists=ctx.output_xlsx.exists(),
            delegate_report=None,
            delegate_returncode=None,
            stdout_tail=stdout_text[-4000:],
            stderr_tail=stderr_text[-4000:],
            notes=[f"delegate execution timed out after {ctx.timeout_seconds} seconds"],
        )
        print(json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 124
    finally:
        try:
            report_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
        except Exception:
            pass

    ok, reasons = _success_from_delegate(delegate_report, ctx.output_xlsx)

    if ok:
        summary = _build_summary(
            status="success",
            ctx=ctx,
            pdf_count=len(pdfs),
            delegated=True,
            output_exists=ctx.output_xlsx.exists(),
            delegate_report=delegate_report,

review_contract.artifact_policy: Always include either `file_contents` for the review artifact path or `artifacts` referencing that exact path.
review_contract.fallback_policy: If evidence is insufficient, return `status=partial`, include explicit `errors`, still generate review artifact content, and set verdict=needs_revision.

constraints: Review must be grounded in produced automation artifact
When both wrapper and reviewed delegate snapshots are supplied, evaluate them as one end-to-end readonly pair instead of ignoring the delegate evidence.
Do not invent missing artifact content
Return a clear verdict: pass, fail, or needs_revision
Generate review artifact markdown for expected_outputs[0].path
Use artifact_snapshots when provided; avoid claiming access problems if snapshot content exists.
When both the generated wrapper and reviewed delegate snapshots are provided, review them together rather than silently narrowing scope to one file.
Return metadata.verdict with one of: pass, fail, needs_revision.
Always generate review artifact content for expected_outputs[0].path.
If evidence is insufficient, use partial + errors + verdict=needs_revision, but still output review artifact.

source.title: BL-20260324-014 live preview smoke sample 2026-03-24 (best-effort reviewable attempt)

acceptance_criteria: Produce a review artifact with explicit verdict (pass/fail/needs_revision)

result.summary: Reviewed the inbox runner and paired delegate together as an end-to-end readonly smoke path. The pair is generally aligned with the best-effort/evidence-backed contract, but the wrapper assumes delegate support for --report-json that the reviewed delegate does not implement, creating a real integration defect that can cause wrapper-reported failure even when the delegate otherwise works. Verdict: needs_revision.
notes: Review grounded in provided artifact snapshots for both scripts.
Scope evaluated as wrapper + delegate pair, not as isolated files.
Review artifact content is included in file_contents.
